titulo_es: Embajada en Londres transmite la moción parlamentaria 'Spanish Democracy' firmada por más de 200 diputados británicos
titulo_en: London Embassy transmits 'Spanish Democracy' parliamentary motion signed by more than 200 British MPs
resumen_es: Nota de la Embajada de España en Londres, firmada por Fernando Arias-Salgado (25 marzo 1981), al Ministerio de Asuntos Exteriores, transmitiendo la moción parlamentaria 'Spanish Democracy' presentada en la Cámara de los Comunes del Reino Unido (nº 357, 16 marzo 1981). La moción, registrada en el Libro de Preguntas y Avisos del Parlamento, fue suscrita por más de doscientos diputados de todos los partidos y expresa la admiración del Parlamento Británico por la actuación del pueblo y las instituciones españolas en defensa de la democracia durante el intento de golpe del 23-F.
resumen_en: Note from Spain's London Embassy, signed by Fernando Arias-Salgado (25 March 1981), to the Ministry of Foreign Affairs, transmitting the 'Spanish Democracy' parliamentary motion tabled in the UK House of Commons (no. 357, 16 March 1981). The motion, recorded in the Parliament's Questions and Notices Book, was signed by more than two hundred MPs from all parties and expresses the British Parliament's admiration for the actions of the Spanish people and institutions in defence of democracy during the 23-F coup attempt.

personas.rol: Embajador de España en Londres, firmante de la nota de transmisión

organizaciones.nombre: Cámara de los Comunes del Reino Unido
Ministerio de Asuntos Exteriores de España

eventos.descripcion_es: La Cámara de los Comunes registra la moción 'Spanish Democracy' (nº 357) con más de 200 firmas de diputados de todos los partidos
eventos.descripcion_en: The House of Commons registers the 'Spanish Democracy' motion (no. 357) with over 200 signatures from MPs of all parties

eventos.descripcion_es: La Embajada de España en Londres transmite la moción al Ministerio de Asuntos Exteriores de España
eventos.descripcion_en: Spain's Embassy in London transmits the motion to the Spanish Ministry of Foreign Affairs

Mr Arthur Davidson Miss Joan Maynard : Mr John Golding |
Mr Greville Janner Mr R. B. Cant
That this House expresses its profound relief and satisfaction that the recent attempt
at coup d’état in Spain was defeated, and recognises that King Juan Carlos played a vital
role in defending the institutions of Spanish democracy, thereby preventing a civil war ;
is keenly aware, however, that Spain’s democracy remains vulnerable to further military
«adventures ; upholds the right of the Spanish people to live under the democratic constitu-
tion approved by referendum ; will do everything in its power to preserve and protect
democracy and the right of all Spaniards to live in peace and liberty and further supports
the efforts of all democratic and parliamentary forces in Spain to safeguard and strengthen
their country’s democratic institutions.
259 BUDGET MEASURES
‘Mr John Loveridge
Sir William Clark
Mr Michael Grylls
Mr Maurice Macmillan
Mr Edward du Cann
Sir John Eden E ed
ade SES Xx 109
Mr John Wheeler Mr Kenneth Carlisle Mr Philip Holland
Mr Eric Cockeram Mr John Spence Mr Reg Prentice
Mr Neil Thorne Mr Allan Stewart
That this House congratulates the Chancellor of the Exchequer ¢ on bringing forward
in his Budget a bold package of measures designed to stimulate investment in the small
and medium business sectors ; and looks forward to further measures to encourage even
wider investment as soon as he feels the nation can afford this.
*& The figure following this symbol gives the total number of names of Members appended, Bis those
names added in this edition of the Notices of Questions and Motions.

Dear Sir,
I am writing to convey to you our great relief and satisfaction that
the recent attempt at coup d'etat in Spain was defeated. We believe
the King Juan Carlos played a vital role in defending the institutions
of Spanish democracy and preventing a return to civil war.
- We believe, ies, that threats to Spain’ s elected government still .
remain. For our part we are watching carefully the political situation
in Spain and we are prepared to do everything in our power to preserve
and protect democracy and the odia of working people to Edy organise
in Spain.
We uphold the right of the Spanish people to live under the democratic
constitution they have approved in a referendum. There must be no
return to Franco. We fully support the efforts of all democratic and
socialist forces in Spain to safeguard and strengthen the country! Ss
poemas institution.
Yours faithfully,

London, March 25, 1981.,
Subject: Parliament and Peer Reactions
Labor stance in the face of events
To foundations February 23.
GENERAL DIRECTORATE OF pysstenio DE and
FOREIGN POLICY PA
RA EUROPE AND AFFAIRS” to
ATLANTIC. - | LH,
No.: ugh q 9 *
7 Hon. Sir:
ts As a continuation of the telegraphic information
ca sent to V.E. last March 16, attached
I have the honor to send the text of the motion "Spanish -
Democracy" with the list of the 200 deputies from all
the trends that have subscribed to it. This motion continues
open for signature and it is likely to continue receiving more
adhesions.
7 I also refer to V.E. photocopy of the card
To the fact that the General Secretary of the Labor Party, Ron
e/ Hayword, has addressed the Director of the House of Spain in
London expressing its satisfaction at the failure of the -
coup attempt in Spain and expressing his "support for the
Spanish democracy in terms similar to those of the
aforementioned parliamentary motion.
God save Your Excellency many years.
THE AMBASSADOR OF SPAIN
FERNANDO ARIAS=SALGADO
